title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
problem_statement: Given heights representing a histogram, find the largest rectangle area that can be formed.

examples.explanation: The largest rectangle covers heights [5,6] with width 2.

intuition: Use a monotonic stack to find the next smaller bar on both sides for each height.

approaches.name: Monotonic stack with boundaries
approaches.description: Push bars onto a stack while heights increase; when a shorter bar appears, calculate max area for popped bars.
approaches.why_not: Brute force checking all pairs of bars is O(n^2) and too slow.
interview_commentary: The stack maintains indices of bars with increasing height to compute widths efficiently.
When a shorter bar appears, calculate areas for taller bars that end at this position.
A sentinel zero height flushes remaining bars and simplifies edge handling.

interview_qa.q: Why add a sentinel zero height?
interview_qa.a: To flush the stack and compute areas for all remaining bars.

interview_qa.q: How is width computed for a popped bar?
interview_qa.a: It is the distance between the current index and the previous smaller bar index minus one.
